title: Mbappé, the French team's adored and criticized superstar
subhed: The captain of Les Bleus, who begin their quest for a third World Cup title on Tuesday, boasts impressive statistics for both club and country. However, in recent years, his public image has declined.

After the US-Iran deal, Israel revels in its military might but finds itself at a strategic impasse
subhed: While Israel aimed to eliminate the 'existential threat' posed by the Iranian regime, it was sidelined from the memorandum of understanding reached Sunday between the US and Iran. Meanwhile, its military now risks losing its freedom of action in Lebanon.

title: Europe tries to weigh in on US-Iran peace process at the G7 in France
subhed: As 60 days of negotiations begin following the official signing of the memorandum of understanding between Washington and Tehran on Friday, European leaders want to avoid an outcome that is too favorable to Iran.

title: JR's new and improved Pont-Neuf cavern reopens
subhed: The artist announced on social media on Monday that his ephemeral work, 'La Caverne du Pont-Neuf,' was reopening three days ahead of schedule. It was repaired and strengthened after strong winds had damaged it two weeks ago.

title: 'The French presidential campaign is unfolding with no set rules or referee to enforce them'
subhed: Except for hard-left La France Insoumise, which is already gearing up for the 2027 presidential election, no party or political camp has escaped the destabilizing effects of an ongoing political realignment that is far from over, according to Le Monde columnist Françoise Fressoz.

title: At Dior, Jonathan Anderson is thinking long-term, not 'trendy'
subhed: One year after this arrival at Dior, the 41-year-old Northern Irish designer is still going at full throttle, releasing collections for Uniqlo and his own brand JW Anderson in addition to his 10 for the luxury label. His goal, he says, is to 'transform' the brand.

title: Universal basic income, the utopian idea resurging in Silicon Valley
subhed: As artificial intelligence threatens to wipe out jobs, the idea of a basic income paid to everyone without conditions is resurfacing. But do tech CEOs share the same motivations as the philosophers and economists who championed it?
